messages.content: Conditions in Jos, Nigeria are stressful for sorghum right now because temperatures are very high. Apply mulch around sorghum seedlings to keep the root zone cooler and retain soil moisture. Watch for heat stress and leaf scorch if temperatures stay high during the first two weeks after planting.

The soil in Marrakech, Morocco is more acidic than ideal for banana, but temperature and moisture are otherwise workable. Apply lime to the soil to raise pH before planting so banana can absorb nutrients properly. Watch for yellowing leaves during the vegetative stage, which signals poor nutrient absorption in acidic soils.

Conditions in Tamale, Ghana are suitable for sorghum planting given the drizzle weather and adequate soil moisture. Incorporate a phosphorus fertilizer into the topsoil to support strong early root development for sorghum. Watch for pest activity, which tends to increase in drizzle conditions — inspect leaves regularly.

The soil in Gulu, Uganda is more acidic than ideal for chili pepper, but temperature and moisture are otherwise workable. Apply lime to the soil to raise pH before planting so chili pepper can absorb nutrients properly. Watch for yellowing leaves during the vegetative stage, which signals poor nutrient absorption in acidic soils.

Watch for heat stress and leaf scorch if temperatures stay high during the first two weeks after planting.

Overall, with good crop management, soybean should establish well in Dakar, Senegal this season.

Conditions in Nakuru, Kenya are suitable for banana planting given the overcast weather and adequate soil moisture. While Hawassa, Ethiopia has plenty of rain, the soil is too wet for soybean planting right now. Wait two to three days for the soil to drain, then prepare raised beds before planting soybean. Watch for root rot and fungal disease if waterlogging continues for more than a few days. Overall, with good crop management, soybean should establish well in Hawassa, Ethiopia this season.
